title: TransferCase
description: A scenario testing whether the self (S = C·P·O·B·K·N) is preserved, destroyed, branched, or only pattern-copied through a substrate or state transition. Transfer(X_t → Y_t+1) preserves S iff it preserves C, P, O, B, K, and N.

pattern_survives.description: Does informational / functional / behavioral pattern survive? Pattern survival is necessary but not sufficient for self-continuity.

c_survives.description: Does fielded appearance (C) survive? null = cannot be established with current tools.

field_continuity_mechanism.description: What mechanism, if any, would carry the phenomenal field across the transition? If none proposed, state 'none proposed.'

s_survives.description: Does the full self (S = C·P·O·B·K·N) survive? null = unresolvable.

components_preserved.description: Which S-components are preserved (true), lost (false), or unknown (null) after the transfer.

branching_occurs.description: Does the transfer create more than one equal heir to the ownership-stream? If true, N collapses to 0.

portable_carrier_required.description: What would need to be true for this transfer to preserve S? Describes the hypothesized portable self-continuity carrier or states 'none proposed.'

classification.description: Classification verdict for this transfer scenario.

revision_pressure.description: What the theory must clarify or revise to handle this case.

breaks_preserved.description: Unresolved breaks this transfer case reveals.

open_questions.description: Questions this case raises that the theory cannot yet answer.
